tipRules.tip: Robertson: Throw a lipless crankbait in grass during the prespawn period on Tennessee River lakes — use it as a primary approach before switching to a big swimbait for bonus bites.
Robertson: After working a lipless bait through grass during prespawn, switch to headhunting with a big swimbait for individual big bites — a two-pronged approach.

knowledge.insight: Robertson: On heavily pressured Tennessee River lakes, wind is the great equalizer — when the wind blows, the fish bite. Root for bad weather on pressured fisheries.
Robertson: A cold snap during prespawn can work in your favor by keeping fish offshore and slowing the spawn, extending the prespawn feeding window.
Robertson: Commit to fishing your strengths — if you come in with no fish, that's fine because you fished how you wanted. Don't chase other patterns.
